data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration (8937's surfaces are real and the schema fields it pins are live producer writes AND consumer reads):
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot exists (fetcher.py:480) and is edited only by 8937 (producer-validates-vs-schema); no sibling co-edits it.
- The dual outbound applier state 33d0 collapses is repo-confirmed: typed leaf _apply_outbound_update is registered at reconcile.py:75 / typed_dispatch.py:56, while production routes through the batch path (batch_dispatch.py:387-388 comment: "the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf _apply_outbound_update"). 8937's round-trip drives "...→ applier through the PRODUCTION path" — i.e. the batch path that is already production today.

File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py (new), fetcher.py (producer-validation). DISJOINT from every sibling: fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). 8937 declares depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's three files are net-new or solely-owned (fetcher.py). The FakeAcliClient/fixtures are PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 8937 through fetch_snapshot — clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write co-edited with any sibling.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins the INBOUND read-side snapshot-entry shapes (comment nested / issuelinks / parent / scalars); fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) feeds exactly those shapes — mutually reinforcing with 59ef (return-shape fidelity) and 822a (behavioral round-trip of the same shapes). The one real cross-child representation conflict in this epic — the outbound labels_applied list-vs-count mismatch — is correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing; 8937's read-side snapshot schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied, so it is untouched here.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937's only input dependency (fe57's fixtures/fake) is declared. Its round-trip "...→ applier" binds to "the production path" ABSTRACTLY: against current code the batch path is already production (and carries comment/link/parent dispatch), and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction — so no hard 33d0-before-8937 edge is required for the test to be meaningful. This is the same abstract-production-path-binding pattern ruled benign for 822a, applied symmetrically here. Applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359), fixture capture (→fe57) are all explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Four stories drive "the production path" from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_acliclient_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only multi-sub-op APPLICATION correctness (apply suite); 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative. The documented anti-change-detector guardrail is assigned to 8937 by NAME in the parent AC ("owned by story B") and in 8937's own description + AC5 — so it is an in-scope deliverable of THIS child, not an overlap with any sibling.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937 does not edit the applier files, so it is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / "no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling is forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (fetch_snapshot writes + inbound/outbound differ reads). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier (_apply_outbound_update), but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION (batch) path, not the typed leaf, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE on the documented anti-change-detector guardrail: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause and 8937's own description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact") + 8937 AC5, this artifact is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) — it is an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency for this pairing.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
